outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Republicans cancel war powers vote after bipartisan majority would have passed it
outlets.summary: GOP leadership pulled a scheduled floor vote on a War Powers Resolution that would have passed with bipartisan support, requiring Trump to seek Congressional authorization to continue operations against Iran. Democrats called it cowardly.

outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Trump keeps changing his timeline for ending the Iran war
outlets.summary: Analysis frames Trump's Iran strategy as erratic — threatening imminent strikes, then calling them off at Gulf allies' request, now saying he's 'in no hurry' while insisting Iran has only 'days' to decide.

outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: 60% of Americans now oppose Trump's war on Iran as conflict enters Day 84
outlets.summary: A new US opinion poll published on Day 84 shows 60% of Americans oppose the Iran war, up significantly from earlier majority-support — reflecting mounting war-weariness as negotiations drag on.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Al Arabiya leaks final draft US-Iran deal — but experts warn it omits nuclear demands
outlets.summary: Al Arabiya published what it claims is a final draft Pakistan-mediated US-Iran agreement. Critics noted the deal as described omits nuclear program dismantlement, HEU export, and ballistic missile limits — all of Trump's stated war objectives.

outlets.name: Breitbart
outlets.headline: Rubio: Iran's Hormuz tolling system would make any deal 'unfeasible' — dealbreaker declared
outlets.summary: Rubio explicitly rejected Iran's plan to charge vessels for Hormuz transit, calling it 'unacceptable' and a deal-killer — narrowing the path to agreement even as both sides signal cautious optimism.

outlets.name: The Hill
outlets.headline: Israel pushes for war amid US ceasefire, but its options may be limited
outlets.summary: Analysis shows Netanyahu's desire to resume war is constrained by US opposition — former Israeli adviser: 'This stops when the US says it stops.' Netanyahu needs a victory before Israeli elections.

outlets.name: Bloomberg
outlets.headline: Iran, Oman in Talks Over Permanent Toll System for Hormuz — Institutionalizing Iran Control
outlets.summary: Iran and Oman are in active talks to formalize a permanent tolling system for Hormuz passage, institutionalizing Iranian control before any peace deal. The ~M/transit PGSA toll mechanism has been operational since May 18. Rubio called it an unfeasible dealbreaker.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: No deal reached yet but gaps narrowed, Iranian source says — Pakistan Naqvi in Tehran
outlets.summary: Iranian source to The National: gaps have been narrowed but no final agreement. Pakistan's Naqvi carried new US proposal to Araghchi and Pezeshkian; Munir's planned visit reportedly cancelled over sticking point.

outlets.name: IEA / CNBC
outlets.headline: IEA warns oil markets could enter 'red zone' by July-August if Hormuz stays closed
outlets.summary: IEA Executive Director Birol warned that falling global inventories, blocked Middle Eastern exports, and rising summer travel demand could converge to create an oil market crisis by mid-summer.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Hezbollah launches 105 attack waves — most intense day since Feb 28 war start
outlets.summary: Hezbollah launched a record 105 attack waves on Israel on May 22, the highest single-day count since the war began. Rockets killed 1 in Nahariya, 2 IDF soldiers killed in Lebanon, 9 soldiers wounded overnight. Lebanon toll reaches 3,079+.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: 4 killed, 2 medics wounded in Israeli strikes in Lebanon's Tyre district overnight
outlets.summary: Israeli strikes continued in south Lebanon overnight despite the 45-day ceasefire extension. Lebanese NNA reported four killed and two medical workers wounded in the Tyre district.

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: IDF kills 2 armed suspects near Rosh Hanikra; sirens twice in northern Israel
outlets.summary: IDF forces shot and killed two armed suspects approaching the Lebanon border near Rosh Hanikra. Two separate air-raid sirens warned of hostile aircraft infiltration near the northern border early May 22.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Mojtaba Khamenei orders Iran's enriched uranium must stay inside country — defies Trump demand
outlets.summary: Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei reportedly issued a directive that Iran's ~440 kg of near-weapons-grade uranium must remain inside Iran and cannot be transferred abroad under any framework — directly blocking Trump's central demand.

outlets.name: IRNA
outlets.headline: Iran ISNA: Both sides exchanging messages and draft texts — 'intense mediation activity' underway
outlets.summary: Iran's state news agency ISNA confirmed ongoing mediated dialogue with Washington, with both sides exchanging draft framework texts. A senior Iranian official said a deal was 'close' per Al Jazeera.

outlets.name: Iran International
outlets.headline: Araghchi: Iran ready for talks or to fight — 'no military solution to a political crisis'
outlets.summary: Araghchi maintained Iran's dual posture: ready to negotiate or resume fighting, while framing Hormuz events as proof that military coercion cannot resolve the core political dispute.
